sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁמֹר-

(1) to keep, to watch, to guard-(a) in a narrower sense, as a garden, Gen 2:15 3:24 a flock, Gen 30:31 a house, Ecc 12:3. Part. שֹׁמֵר subst. a watchman Canticles 3:3 of cattle, i.e. a shepherd, 1Sa 17:20 trop. used of prophets, Isa 21:11 62:6 , compare צֹפִים.-(b) in a wider sense, to keep safe, to preserve, followed by acc. Job 2:6 Pro 13:3 בְּ 2Sa 18:12 אֶל 1Sa 26:15 עַל 1Sa 26:16 Pro 6:22 often used of God as guarding men, followed by an acc. Gen 28:15, 20 Gen 28:20 Psa 12:8 16:1 25:20 followed by מִן to guard from any thing, Psa 121:7 140:5 141:9 .

(2) to keep, to reserve, Exo 22:6 also to preserve, as loving-kindness, Dan 9:4 Neh 9:32, anger, Amo 1:11, עֶבְרָתֹו שְׁמָרָה נֶצַח “(Edom) kept his anger continually;” (שְׁמָֽרָה with these vowels and the accent on the penultima is masc. with ה parag.), and without the acc. עֶבְרָה or אַף (like נָטַר No. 2), Jer 3:5, אִם־יִשְׁמֹר לָנֶצַח “will he continually keep” sc. his anger? Specially to keep in mind and memory (φυλάττεσθαί τι), Gen 37:11 Psa 130:3.-Without acc. and with suff. of pers. Job 10:14, וּשְׁמַרְתָּנִי “thou wilt keep (punishment) for me,” du gedachteft (es) mir.

(3) to observe, to attend to any thing, followed by an acc. 1Sa 1:12 Psa 17:4, “I have observed the ways of the violent man,” i.e. that I might avoid them (this phrase is used in another sense, Pro 2:20); without this, Isa 42:20 followed by עַל Job 14:16 followed by אֶל Psa 59:10. Sometimes used in a bad sense, to watch narrowly (etwas belauern), to lie in wait for, followed by an acc. Job 13:27 33:11 Psa 56:7 71:10 . שָׁמַר עִיר to observe, i.e. to besiege a city, 2Sa 11:16, compare נָצַר No. 5.

(4) to keep, to observe, as a covenant, Gen 17:9, 10 Gen 17:10the commandments of God, 1Ki 11:10 the sabbath, Isa 56:2, Isa 56:6a promise, 1Ki 3:6 8:24 . Followed by a gerund, to seek to do any thing, Num 23:12 2Ki 10:31.

(5) to honour, to worship, as God, Hos 4:10, idols, Psa 31:7 a master, Pro 27:18. Compare Virg. Georg. iv. 212, “Prœterea regem non sic Ægyptus, et ingens Lydia … … observant.”

(6) recipr. i.q. Niphal and שָׁמַר נַפְשֹׁו ( Deu 4:9), to abstain oneself from any thing, followed by מִן Jos 6:18.

Niphal-

(1) pass. to be kept, preserved, Psa 37:28.

(2) to abstain oneself from any thing (compare Kal No. 6), followed by מִן Deu 23:10 Jud. 13:13 1Sa 21:5.

(3) to beware of any thing, followed by מִן Jer 9:3 מִפְּנֵי Exo 23:21 בְּ 2Sa 20:10 followed by inf. Exo 19:12, “take heed to yourselves to ascend the mountain,” i.e. that ye do not ascend; also פֶּן (lest) followed by an entire sentence, Gen 24:6 31:24 , 29 Gen 31:29 Deu 4:15 11:16 (the imperative having sometimes added the pronoun pleon. לְךָ Gen. Ex. locc. citt.). Sometimes, to make the prohibition more forcible, there is added to the verb of warning the expression לְנַפְשְׁךָ, בְּנַפְשְׁךָ by thy life (Germ. bey Leibe nicht), which does not depend on the verb נִשְׁמַר, Deu 4:15, 16 , נִשְׁמַרְתֶּם מְאֹד לְנַפְשֹׁותֵיכֶם פֶּן תַּשְׁחִיתוּ “take heed diligently, as ye love your life, not to act wickedly,” etc.; Jer 17:21, הִשָּֽׁמְרוּ בְּנַפְשׁתֵיכֶם וְאַל תִּשְּׂאוּ “take heed as ye value your life, not to carry;” Jos 23:11. Once followed by a gerund, to care for something, to take heed to do something, Deu 24:8, הִשָּׁמֶר … לִשְׁמֹר מְאֹד וְלַעֲשֹׂות “take heed to observe diligently and do,” etc.

Piel, i.q. Kal No. 5, to worship (an idol), Jon 2:9.

Hithpael-

(1) i.q. Kal No. 4, prop. to observe for oneself, Mic 6:16.

(2) to take heed to oneself, followed by מִן Psa 18:24.

Derivatives, שֶׁמֶר-שִׁמְרָת, אַשְׁמֻרָה, מִשְׁמָר, מִשְׁמֶרֶת, and pr.n. יִשְׁמְרַי.

II.

שמר

i.q. סָמַר, שָׁמַר Ch. Pa. סַמֵּר to fasten with nails; whence שָׁמִיר a thorn, a point. Perhaps the two significations (No. I., II.) may be reconciled from the sense of guarding, coming from that of shutting up, making fast with nails.
